fact: April 11 is exactly 101 days into the year - that's a palindrome number that reads the same forwards and backwards!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for cherry blossoms to explode into pink clouds that make entire cities look like they're covered in cotton candy.
Spring babies born around this time have perfectly timed their arrival with the warmest weather and most food in northern climates.
The sun rises almost exactly due east and sets due west around April 11 in most places - nature's perfect compass!
Earthworms go absolutely crazy this time of year, wiggling up from deep soil as the ground warms up, making lawns look like spaghetti factories exploded.
April 11 falls smack in the middle of Aries season, when the zodiac's ram is supposedly making everyone extra headstrong and energetic.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date marks the heart of autumn when leaves turn brilliant orange and red like nature's own fireworks show.
Dandelions transform from sunny yellow flowers into those magical white puff balls around this time - each one holding up to 2,000 tiny parachute seeds ready to fly!
Tree sap runs like crazy in early April - maple syrup makers are working overtime because trees are basically sugar fountains right now!
Migrating butterflies, especially Monarchs in North America, are hitting their stride on their epic journeys, traveling up to 100 miles per day around this time.
April 11 sits exactly 74 days before the summer solstice - close enough that daylight is stretching noticeably longer each day in the north, adding about 2-3 minutes of light daily.
